findings.signature: db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL') with fallback to DELETE on SQLITE_READONLY/CANTOPEN/IOERR
findings.notes: WAL mode pragma with file-system error fallback; Turso has no .pragma() API — this entire pattern must be replaced or removed.
Foreign key enforcement via pragma; Turso enables FKs by default but lacks .pragma() — must verify Turso FK behavior or remove call.
WAL checkpoint is a local-file-only concept; Turso/libSQL uses different replication — this entire function is migration-blocking dead code.

findings.signature: database.exec(`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ... CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS ...`)
findings.notes: Multi-statement .exec() DDL; Turso supports this but schema migration pattern couples to synchronous better-sqlite3 .exec() semantics.
PRAGMA table_info for column detection during migration; Turso lacks PRAGMA introspection — must use sqlite_master parsing or information_schema.
File-level destructive recovery (rename/rm the .sqlite file); Turso DB is a remote service — file-based corruption recovery is impossible, needs service-level replacement.

findings.signature: const tx = database.transaction(() => { deleteMissingNodes + upsertNode + deleteEdgesBySource + insertEdge })
findings.notes: Synchronous transaction wrapper over better-sqlite3; Turso/libSQL JS client uses async transactions — this entire block must become async.

findings.signature: INSERT INTO skill_graph_metadata ... ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET
findings.notes: Upsert via ON CONFLICT; Turso supports this but synchronous .prepare().run() pattern must become async.

findings.signature: INSERT INTO ${tableName} (skill_id, embedding, ...) ON CONFLICT(skill_id) DO UPDATE SET
findings.notes: Dynamic table name (vec_<dim>) with embedding BLOB upsert; Turso lacks vec0 virtual tables and vector indexes — the entire adapter path is migration-blocking.

findings.signature: new Database(databasePath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true })
findings.notes: Read-only mode via better-sqlite3 constructor flag; Turso connections are inherently read-replicas — the readonly concept maps differently.
WAL fallback error detection pattern; Turso has no WAL mode — this error-handling convention is migration-blocking dead code.

findings.signature: db.exec(`PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL; CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skill_graph_daemon_lease ...`)
findings.notes: Lease DB sets WAL mode inline with DDL in a single .exec() call; Turso has no PRAGMA journal_mode — lease DB must use a different concurrency model entirely.

findings.signature: const reserve = db.transaction((): LeaseAcquireResult => { SELECT + INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE })
findings.notes: Heartbeat-based lease acquisition in synchronous transaction; the entire single-writer lease model assumes local-file WAL locking — Turso's remote locking model invalidates this design.

findings.signature: UPDATE skill_graph_daemon_lease SET heartbeat_at = ?, pid = ? WHERE workspace_key = ? AND owner_id = ?
findings.notes: Periodic heartbeat UPDATE on lease row; if Turso connection drops, heartbeat fails silently — needs connection-liveness detection absent in current code.

findings.signature: DELETE FROM skill_graph_daemon_lease WHERE workspace_key = ? AND owner_id = ?
findings.notes: Lease release via DELETE; Turso supports DELETE but the lease model's correctness depends on local-file atomicity guarantees that Turso remote connections may not provide.

findings.signature: function runDaemonStateMutation<T>(dbPath, initialize, mutate, attempts=3): T with SQLITE_BUSY retry loop
findings.notes: Generic SQLITE_BUSY retry wrapper opening a fresh DB per attempt; Turso has different error codes for contention — the retry predicate must be updated.

findings.signature: new Database(dbPath) — no WAL, no busy_timeout, no pragmas
findings.notes: Opens daemon state DB with zero pragmas (no WAL, no busy_timeout); relies on caller retry logic — Turso connection pooling semantics would change this.
PRAGMA quick_check is a local-file integrity probe; Turso/libSQL remote has no equivalent — this entire module is migration-blocking dead code.

findings.signature: new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true }) + db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000')
findings.notes: Integrity probe opens read-only with busy_timeout pragma; Turso connections don't support .pragma() — must wrap with Turso-specific connection config.
